sessions.intent: Migrate hand-built goal/step executor tests in PLang.Tests/Modules to load goals through the real read path (TestApp.Create + Make.Goal + RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel).

before.assumptions: Baseline PLang.Tests/Modules is green (failed: 0). Executor tests are those calling _app.RunGoalAsync or step(s).RunAsync on a hand-built goal/step; handler-direct .Run()/action.RunAsync and provider/fold isolation tests stay.
before.risk: ViaChannel serialize->read round-trip may expose a real read-path gap on some param shape (e.g. goal.call goalname dict). If so, revert that file and report.

actions.context: Migrated step.RunAsync executor tests: new app->TestApp.Create; runner step built via Make.Goal + RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel, capture goal.Steps.First() then step.RunAsync. foreach itemname/keyname -> Make.Param(...,'variable'). Empty target goals (DoNothing/Noop/ProcessItem) and the registered Inner fixture left as new Goal. The 3 action.RunAsync-direct foreach tests (Empty/Null/Cancellation) left as-is.

StepsSubStep: Step-returning helpers -> Make.StepDef helpers + LoadSteps() routes through ViaChannel, returning goal.Steps for steps.RunAsync (and HasIndentedChildren). IfErrorOrchestration: removed now-unused Action alias; both step.RunAsync tests load via ViaChannel.

3 RunGoalAsync executor tests (Read_UnregisteredScheme, Integration_FileExists, Integration_FileNotExists) now build via Make.Goal + ViaChannel; dropped manual StampTemplates walk. variable.set Name -> Make.Param('Name',...,'variable'). All .Run() handler-direct tests left as-is. Constructor new app -> TestApp.Create.

Examined, deliberately left: all execute via handler .Run()/action.RunAsync/modifiers.RunAsync/_provider.Render — isolation tests, not RunGoalAsync/steps.RunAsync executors. RenderTests goals are callGoal registry fixtures, never directly run.

after.notes: PLang.Tests/Modules builds clean (no error CS) and full suite passes (failed: 0). No read-path gaps exposed; nothing reverted. Not committed (per task).

sessions.intent: Codeanalyzer review of template-stamping-at-read: OBP + simplicity + behavioral + root-cause passes, prioritized by risk (handoff B1-B5 + scalars-as-native item apex).

before.assumptions: First codeanalyzer review on this branch. Branch carries the entire scalars-as-native refactor (527 commits off runtime2) plus the recent template-stamping production changes (handoff B1-B5). Coder claims all six C# suites green at handoff.
before.risk: 361 production C# files changed (+10359/-5157). Full line-by-line coverage in one pass is unrealistic; review is risk-prioritized. Highest risk: security-critical signing/identity reads (B3), the item apex + scalar-type proliferation, the ScalarComparer/Compare->Comparison collapse, and Data.cs core changes.

actions.context: Handoff B1-B5: datetime navigable members, Data.Clr<T>(fallback) lift, system-var typed reads, Variables.GetValue removal, bracket-resolution fix. Ed25519 sign/verify clock-fallback asymmetry reviewed (N1, justified). B5 ResolveVariablesInPath fix sound; minor note F5 (top-frame-only precedence).

scalars-as-native core. item apex OBP-clean (behavior-on-element, storage-free; N5). compare mediator mirrors convert pattern (N2). Findings: F1 dead BothPresent (deletion test), F2 orphaned enum summary in Comparison.cs, F3 two Write methods on item apex (naming).

Mechanical passes (System.IO/Console/Rule-9 courier/provenance) all clean across changed production set. Root-cause pass: both fix() commits (cache JsonElement c27c37c5a, Fluid blank d2d2f557a) are genuine root-cause fixes. F4: Normalize silently reflects unknown raw-CLR leaf to ValueKind bag instead of failing loud — systemic/pre-existing hardening item.

after.notes: VERDICT: PASS on risk-prioritized surface. Build green (0 errors). Findings all LOW except F4 (systemic pre-existing). Non-blocking cleanups for coder: delete dead BothPresent (F1), move orphaned Comparison enum summary (F2). NOT a full certification of the 361-file scalars-as-native body (relies on prior architect/test-designer/coder rounds + green C# suites). Next bot: tester.
